My four year-old grandson and I spent over four hours at this museum and we didn’t see everything this museum features signage in English Spanish and Vietnamese with a variety of indoor and outdoor active hands-on activities. My grandson played for a long time on the fire engine dressing up in the clothes and driving the fire truck and putting out fires fortunately our possibly unfortunately the sirens sounds on the fire truck are not working at this time. We also enjoyed the culturally diverse market area with displays of Filipino coconut graders, Vietnamese techniques, Chinese food prep and a variety of ethnic plastic food for the kids to prepare in the kitchen area with a stove and in the serving area, the dinosaur exhibit is very cool with a dinosaur skeleton, and it shows how the muscles fit over the dinosaur. I noticed that staff people were cleaning several of the toys that children were touching. The outdoor area includes an area dedicated to Bill a pioneer in ecology and recycling. There is a sandbox area for digging. There is a very cool bubble area for blowing bubbles climbing area in a place called Bill’s backyard. There’s the discovery meadow area. 
We took a lunch break in the cafeteria area with sandwiches, yogurt, parfait options and several coffees with an accessible height, order and pick up counter and accessible height tables right near the café are vending machines for additional beverages and snacks. The parking lot offers van accessible paid parking Please note that this parking lot connects with the SAP arena and parking is very expensive for concerts and sporting events. You will find a code for reduced parking price on the children’s museum website the entry to the museum is accessible with a very large field in front of the museum, including picnic tables movement through the museum is accessible many of the exhibits are accessible, except for example, with a climbing apparatus Outside we thoroughly enjoyed this museum. We would’ve stayed much longer than four hours.
They also offer a room for parties men’s and women’s and family accessible restrooms the men’s and women’s restroom offer a dedicated stall with two grab bars and 5‘ x 5‘ space

This is a nice place to spend a morning with a 5 and 7 year old (older kids will be quickly bored).  The water 'park' area was especially engaging for the kids (bring a change of t-shirts), and they liked the 'bubble wrap' bursting event.  But compared to the Adventure Science Center in Nashville, it's rather old, out of date and not especially educational.  It was also very crowded - probably because the weather was so hot.  Clear instructions on where to park would also have been helpful.
